hero.title_html: Finally, SCADA supervision without tag pricing.
hero.subtitle_html: Wiregrid reads your PLCs over Modbus TCP, OPC-UA and BACnet/IP, and writes every measurement into a PostgreSQL database that Power BI, Grafana or Excel read directly. No per-tag licensing. No proprietary connector.
hero.cta_shell_aria: Copy the docker compose up -d command
hero.cta_note_html: Public demo, no sign-up. Auth and data residency available the moment you self-host.
hero.preview_label: Live demo · simulated values (real WebSocket push in the actual demo)

app_preview.aria_label: Wiregrid interface preview: live points table
app_preview.title: Live points

stats.drivers: drivers across Modbus / OPC-UA / BACnet in the demo
stats.points: live points at 1 Hz, sub-100 ms
stats.latency: WebSocket latency between Redis and the browser
stats.tag_cost: per tag — no asterisk, no condition

comparison.title: Compare row by row, not in marketing slides.
comparison.subtitle: Wiregrid is not a Niagara N4 replacement on a 5,000-point site with full wiresheet and a mature HMI builder. It targets the cases where you mostly pay for tags, on features that PostgreSQL + Power BI already do better.
comparison.disclaimer: Public sources, April 2026. Niagara, Ignition, EcoStruxure: trademarks of Tridium, Inductive Automation, Schneider Electric.

how.title: Three steps. One Sunday evening.
how.subtitle: No mandatory integration services. The public demo replicates the install an automation engineer can do without calling us.

1_install.title: Install
1_install.desc: One Docker command on a VPS, an industrial mini-PC or a Raspberry Pi 4. The image bundles the gateway, PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB, Redis and the UI.

2_configure.title: Connect
2_configure.desc: You declare your PLCs by IP and port. Wiregrid lists the exposed points, you organise them by machine and tags, you wire the alarms.

3_operate.title: Operate
3_operate.desc: Values stream over WebSocket into the UI. In parallel your Postgres is open for direct read from Power BI, Grafana, Metabase or Excel.

features.title: Three things classic SCADAs do badly.
features.subtitle: The rest — drivers, alarms, historian, multi-project — also runs in the demo and is listed below. But these three are why you'd look at Wiregrid instead of Niagara or Ignition.

open_data.title_html: Your data in your Postgres, not in ours.
open_data.desc_html: Every config row (drivers, points, machines, alarms…) sits in standard PostgreSQL tables. Value history lives in a TimescaleDB hypertable point_history(ts, point_id, value, quality). Power BI connects through ODBC the same way it would to any enterprise SQL database.
open_data.bullet_1_html: No proprietary connector to buy or install
open_data.bullet_3_html: If Wiregrid disappears, your data and dashboards keep working

live.title_html: 1-2 ms from PLC to browser.
live.desc_html: The runtime publishes every value change to Redis pub/sub. The gateway forwards to the browser via /ws/live. When the WS drops, fallback to 1 s polling with exponential reconnect. Read and write supported on commandable points.
live.bullet_1_html: Direct Redis → WebSocket push, no backend polling

versioning.title_html: Every change is a commit. Every rollback, a transaction.
versioning.desc_html: Editing a setpoint, adding an alarm, deleting a machine — everything goes into a revision. Diff between two revisions, single-transaction revert to a past state, runtime hot-reload via pg_notify. You test on the working copy, you deploy when it's ready.
versioning.bullet_2_html: Atomic revert to any past revision
versioning.bullet_3_html: Full audit log: who, what, when, from which diff

drivers.title: Multi-protocol
drivers.desc_html: Modbus TCP, OPC-UA and BACnet/IP in a single install. Independent polling per device, automatic reconnection. 10 drivers across the 3 protocols in the demo.

alarms.title: Operational alarms
alarms.desc_html: high/low conditions with threshold, deadband and delay. Severities info / warning / critical. Email notifications via user groups.

historian.title: TimescaleDB historian
historian.desc_html: Hypertable chunked by day. Per-point on_change with deadband or fixed-interval mode. Configurable retention, time_bucket() queries for downsampling.

integrations.title: Open API
integrations.desc_html: REST on /py/v1/*, /api/v1/* alias for legacy integrations, WebSocket /ws/live, plus direct SQL access to Postgres.

multitenant.title: Multi-project
multitenant.desc_html: project_id scoping on every entity. The demo signup clones a complete project (10 drivers / 13 machines / 18 alarms) in under a second.

wiresheet.title: Visual programmable logic
wiresheet.desc_html: Niagara N4-style functional block editor: math, comparison, timer, schedule, latch, ramp, PID. The execution engine is already in place — the Vue Flow editor lands in Q3 2026.

equipment.title: Your PLCs already speak Wiregrid.
equipment.subtitle: Three open protocols cover nearly every industrial PLC out there. Representative list — technical nomenclature, not advertising, so no vendor logos.
equipment.note: Any device speaking Modbus TCP, OPC-UA or BACnet/IP is compatible. For a PLC with old firmware, plan a half-day validation. Modbus RTU over RS-485 needs a TCP gateway (Moxa, USR-TCP232, or equivalent).

pricing.title: Two tiers. No per-tag pricing.
pricing.subtitle: The Discovery plan covers a full small site — 50 points already covers most single-building setups a building-automation contractor handles. Beyond that, a flat annual fee.
pricing.footnote: Prices excl. VAT, billed annually. 12-month commitment. You stay the owner of your data and your instance — Wiregrid has no access to your PostgreSQL.

pro.name: Production
pro.anchor_html: ≈ equivalent to 200 Niagara tags, or 1/4 of a Workbench station.

faq.title: The questions we get.
faq.subtitle: If yours isn't here, drop us a line at contact@wiregrid.fr — reply within one business day.

difference_niagara.q: What's the actual difference vs Niagara N4?
difference_niagara.a_html: Niagara is more mature. Full wiresheet, HMI builder, JACE ecosystem, thousands of trained integrators. Wiregrid is smaller, younger, more modern (pure web, no JVM station). And most importantly without tag pricing: the ~€5/tag/year you pay on Niagara, you don't pay with us. Wiregrid sweet spot: 50 to 500 points, not 5,000.

compatible_plc.q: Compatible with my Schneider M340 / Siemens S7-1500 / other PLC?
compatible_plc.a_html: If your PLC speaks Modbus TCP (most Schneider, ABB, Wago, Phoenix Contact), OPC-UA (Siemens S7-1500 natively, Beckhoff TwinCAT, B&R) or BACnet/IP (Distech, Honeywell, Trend, JCI), yes. For Modbus RTU over serial, plan a TCP gateway. See the Compatibility section.

data_format.q: What format is the data stored in?
data_format.a_html: Configuration: standard PostgreSQL tables (drivers, points, machines, alarms…). History: TimescaleDB hypertable point_history(ts, point_id, value, quality), chunked by day. Everything queryable in plain SQL, no proprietary dialect.

powerbi.q: How do I connect Power BI?
powerbi.a_html: Power BI Desktop > Get Data > PostgreSQL. You enter your Wiregrid server's IP (port 5432), a read-only user, and you see the tables. No proprietary connector to install.

self_host.a_html: Self-hosted. docker compose up -d on your server. Your data never leaves your network. The online demo runs on a public environment but it's a demo, not a production offer.

minimum_hardware.a_html: 2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM, 20 GB disk are enough for ~500 points with 30 days of history. Runs on an industrial mini-PC, a Raspberry Pi 4 or a €5/month Hetzner VPS.

security.a_html: JWT auth (UI) or API key (integrations). postgres and redis bind to localhost, never exposed. If you put the gateway behind Caddy, Nginx or a Cloudflare Tunnel, you get automatic TLS.

safety_sil.q: Can I run functional safety (SIL) on it?
safety_sil.a_html: No, and never will. Wiregrid supervises and historises, period. Your safety interlocks must stay in your certified PLC.

open_source.a_html: Not publicly yet. The license will be decided at public release (candidate: AGPL-3.0 or source-available). In the meantime: your data is in your PostgreSQL in standard SQL.

company_disappears.q: What if you shut down tomorrow?
company_disappears.a_html: Fair question — and the main reason Wiregrid stores everything in standard SQL. Concretely: your postgres runs on your server. Your Power BI dashboards keep working. For acquisition, you replace Wiregrid with any other platform speaking Modbus / OPC-UA / BACnet — the equipment-wiring work is done once.

multi_site.a_html: Today: multi-project on a single instance, ideal for multi-building setups within one group. For multi-site with edge gateways, that's on the Q4 2026 roadmap.

wiresheet_status.q: When does the wiresheet (visual logic) ship?
wiresheet_status.a_html: The execution engine is already in the runtime. The Vue Flow editor (block palette, drag-drop, links) is targeted for Q3 2026.

support.a_html: Discovery plan: documentation and public GitHub issues. Production plan: email with a 1-business-day response SLA, video calls on request, direct access to the lead developer.

trial.q: How long do I need to seriously try it?
trial.a_html: The online demo works as an instant POC (60 seconds of clicking around). For a trial on your own equipment, plan 1–2 hours to wire up a PLC and see your real points.

footer.tagline: Open web SCADA supervision. Your Modbus / OPC-UA / BACnet PLCs, your data in standard Postgres, directly readable by Power BI.
footer.newsletter_desc: Roadmap, integrator stories, releases. Four sends a year, no more.
